chapter_context.summary: Elijah challenges the 450 prophets of Baal to a contest on Mount Carmel; the fire of the LORD falls and consumes Elijah's water-soaked sacrifice; the Baal prophets are slain, and a great rain falls.

outline.text: Elijah Commanded to Meet Ahab; Obadiah's Faithfulness
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah Commanded to Meet Ahab; Obadiah's Faithfulness (18:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Elijah Confronts Obadiah; Demands Audience with Ahab
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah Confronts Obadiah; Demands Audience with Ahab (18:7-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Elijah Meets Ahab; Challenges Baal's Prophets to Mount Carmel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah Meets Ahab; Challenges Baal's Prophets to Mount Carmel (18:17-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Baal's Prophets Call in Vain; Elijah Mocks Them
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Baal's Prophets Call in Vain; Elijah Mocks Them (18:20-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Elijah's Prayer; Fire Falls and Consumes Offering; Baal Prophets Slain
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah's Prayer; Fire Falls and Consumes Offering; Baal Prophets Slain (18:30-40), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Elijah Prays for Rain; A Great Rain Falls; Elijah Runs to Jezreel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah Prays for Rain; A Great Rain Falls; Elijah Runs to Jezreel (18:41-46), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 18, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
